message:
feat(core/membrane): add SeqLock<T>, harden bandit/sos_barrier tests, simplify string_abi fast paths

SeqLock<T> in frankenlibc-core/rcu:
- Lock-free sequence-lock for small (<=64 byte) configuration payloads,
  using atomic u64 lane storage to eliminate torn reads without requiring
  a full RCU domain. Readers are wait-free when no writer is active.
  This is needed because runtime-policy configuration is read on every
  intercepted libc call, and the existing RCU domain adds unnecessary
  epoch bookkeeping for data that changes at most once per process lifetime.
- Includes concurrent stress test (4 readers x 1 writer x 50K writes)
  verifying no torn checksums are ever observed.

Bandit router (frankenlibc-membrane/runtime_math):
- Add tests for hardened-mode contention gating and strict-mode exploration
  to ensure the constrained multi-armed bandit correctly gates Full validation
  only in hardened mode, not in strict mode where Fast should remain reachable.
- Add cadence_recompute_prefers_fast test verifying that after a full UCB
  recompute window with clearly dominant Fast utility, the cached profile
  converges correctly.

SOS barrier certificates (frankenlibc-membrane/runtime_math):
- Add positive-semidefiniteness verification for both Gram matrices via
  exhaustive principal minor determinant checking — this is the fundamental
  mathematical property that makes SOS certificates sound.
- Add task source hash verification against checked-in .task artifacts.
- Add saturation tests ensuring quadratic form and barrier evaluators
  handle i64::MAX/u32::MAX inputs without overflow.
- Add monotonicity tests for fragmentation (size dispersion) and thread
  safety (writer pressure) barriers.

string_abi.rs cleanup:
- Remove redundant SafetyLevel-gated fast paths from memcpy/memmove/memset.
  These were bypassing the membrane guard entirely in non-Off modes, which
  defeats the purpose of membrane-gated interposition. The reentrant
  fallback path already handles the recursion-avoidance case correctly,
  making the SafetyLevel check both unnecessary and counterproductive
  (it prevented membrane telemetry from observing these critical hot-path
  calls in strict/hardened modes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
